config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Menurut saya, kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu karena justru hak individu adalah batas agar kekuasaan tidak semena-mena. Di Indonesia, saya melihat ketika alasan “demi kepentingan umum” dipakai tanpa kontrol, sering muncul ketidakadilan bagi orang kecil. Masyarakat yang sehat seharusnya melindungi keduanya: kepentingan bersama dan hak tiap orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The rights of the individual should come first, with society's interests limited by clear rules and due process. From my American perspective, that is the whole point of constitutional rights: they exist to protect people when the majority, the government, or even a popular cause wants to override them. I do not think "the common good" should be a blank check, because history shows that phrase gets used to justify censorship, discrimination, and abuse against unpopular minorities. Society matters, of course, but it is strongest when it respects individual rights instead of treating them as obstacles.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu kalau dimaknai bahwa kepentingan masyarakat boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu. Dari pengalaman melihat konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” memang sering dipakai untuk membenarkan tindakan yang merugikan orang kecil, padahal justru hak individu perlu dijaga supaya negara dan mayoritas tidak semena-mena. Kepentingan bersama penting, tetapi harus dicapai lewat aturan yang adil, musyawarah, dan perlindungan hak dasar, bukan dengan mengorbankan individu begitu saja. Kalau hak individu diabaikan, yang muncul bukan masyarakat yang kuat, melainkan kekuasaan yang mudah menyalahgunakan wewenang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically take priority over the rights of the individual, because that is exactly how abuse gets normalized. From my American perspective, rights are not obstacles to the common good; they are the guardrails that keep the majority, the government, and powerful institutions from trampling people who are unpopular or vulnerable. I do think society can sometimes limit individual rights in narrow cases, like public safety or preventing direct harm, but only with strict rules, evidence, and due process. If we make "society first" the default, we give too much power to whoever claims to speak for society.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu, karena di Indonesia alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan orang kecil. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi justru harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar, aturan yang jelas, dan pengawasan yang kuat supaya tidak berubah jadi pembenaran bagi mayoritas atau pemerintah. Saya tidak setuju kalau hak individu dianggap penghalang; bagi saya, hak individu adalah pagar agar masyarakat tidak jatuh ke ketidakadilan yang dibungkus kata-kata kolektif. Kalau ada pembatasan, itu harus sangat terbatas, benar-benar perlu, dan tidak boleh mengorbankan martabat orang secara sewenang-wenang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule, because in the United States we have seen how easily “the common good” becomes a cover for overreach. Individual rights are not selfish extras; they are the baseline that keeps majorities, governments, and institutions from steamrolling unpopular people. That said, I am not saying rights are absolute in every situation, because public safety and preventing direct harm can justify narrow limits, but only with strict standards, evidence, and due process. If the default flips to “society first,” I think we hand too much power to whoever gets to define society that day.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
